questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Space
questions.questionText: Varkala, Valiamala, Bekal and Thumba have been recently in the news for which one of the following reasons?

options.a:
They have been selected as the new
locations for ISRO’s Small Satellite Launch Programme.
options.b:
They have been assigned as official names
for certain Martian surface features by the International Astronomical Union.
options.c:
They are the potential sites of the Thorium
reserves in India.
options.d:
They are the official names for India’s
Nuclear-Powered Attack Submarines to be made under Project-77.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **International Astronomical Union (IAU)** has approved a 3.5 billion-year-old crater on Mars to be known after **M.S. Krishnan**, the pioneering Indian geologist.
● Apart from ‘Krishnan,’ the IAU has also accepted several Kerala-based names for **smaller landforms** **associated with the crater**. These are ‘Valiamala,’ ‘Thumba,’ ‘Bekal,’ ‘Varkala’ and ‘Periyar’ for smaller craters and a vallis (valley). Which means, these places in Kerala now have counterparts on Mars.
● Dr. Krishnan was the first Indian to be director of the Geological Society of India. As for the other names, it is the first time that place names from Kerala are being adopted for Martian features.
● While Valiamala is home to the IIST, Thumba is the place where the Indian space programme had its origins in the 1960s. Varkala is known for its geologically unique cliff formations and considered a potential Martian analogue site. Bekal in Kasaragod district was chosen for the historic Bekal Fort. Periyar is Kerala’s longest river, and is now commemorated through the naming of a Martian valley (Vallis). **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the International Astronomical Union (IAU) has approved names of Kerala localities — Valiamala, Thumba, Bekal, Varkala and Periyar — as well as of noted geologist M S Krishnan, to identify various craters on Mars.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Industry (Waste Materials, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. - Wastewater treatment
II.	 Food and beverage industry
III.	 Pharmaceutical manufacturing
IV.	 Pesticide and herbicide application
How many of the above are the applications of Silicone-based defoamers?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Defoamers** are essential additives used to eliminate or suppress foam in various industrial processes. Among the most common types are silicone-based and polyether-based defoamers, each with unique properties and application scenarios.
● **Silicone defoamers** are primarily composed of silicone oils (such as polydimethylsiloxane) and emulsifying agents, sometimes blended with hydrophobic silica and other surfactants. Thanks to their molecular structure, they can be used in both aqueous (water-based) and non-aqueous (oil-based) systems.
● **Effluent Treatment Plants (ETPs):** In **wastewater treatment** facilities, the control of foam is essential. Silicone defoamers are extensively used to manage foam generated during the treatment process, ensuring the smooth operation of ETPs. **So, point I is correct.**
● **Food and Beverage:** The **food and beverage industry** faces challenges related to foam in processes such as brewing, fermentation, and food production. Silicone defoamers are crucial in preventing foam-related issues and maintaining product integrity. **So, point II is correct.**
● **Pharmaceuticals:** In **pharmaceutical manufacturing**, precise control over the production process is crucial. Silicone defoamers help prevent foam formation during drug formulation, ensuring accurate dosing and product quality. **So, point III is correct.**
● **Agriculture:** In agricultural practices like pesticide and herbicide application, foam can interfere with the effectiveness of the chemicals. Silicone defoamers are used to minimize foam formation, ensuring proper coverage and product efficacy. **So, point IV is correct.** **So, all four of the above are the applications of Silicone-based defoamers.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
**Other applications of Silicone-based defoamers:**
● **Construction:** The construction industry utilizes silicone defoamers in various applications, including in concrete production and drilling fluids, where foam control is crucial for successful outcomes.
● **Cosmetics:** The cosmetics industry relies on precise formulations to create products with the desired texture and appearance. Silicone defoamers help manage foam in various cosmetic formulations.
● **Mining and Mineral Processing:** Foam can be a challenge in the mining and mineral processing industry, affecting the efficiency of separation processes. Silicone defoamers aid in reducing foam and improving mineral recovery.
● **Oil and Gas:** In the oil and gas sector, foam can hinder the separation of oil, gas, and water. Silicone defoamers are employed to control foam in production processes, ensuring efficient separation and product quality.
● **Paints and Coatings:** Foam in paint and coating formulations can lead to uneven application and reduced quality. Silicone defoamers are added to these formulations to maintain a smooth and even finish.
● **Pulp and Paper Industry:** Foam can be a common issue in pulp and paper manufacturing, affecting paper quality and machine efficiency. Silicone defoamers are utilized to combat foam and ensure the smooth production of paper products.
● **Textile Industry:** Textile processes, such as dyeing and printing, can lead to foam buildup. Silicone defoamers assist in managing foam, ensuring consistent and highquality textile products.
● **Chemical Manufacturing:** Chemical processes often involve the generation of foam, which can impede efficient production. Silicone defoamers are employed to eliminate foam in various chemical manufacturing applications, improving productivity and product quality.
**Relevance:** Recently, there have been concerns raised over the prolonged use of silicone-based defoamers to prevent frothing in the Yamuna river in Delhi.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: Money (Evolution, Types, Digital Currency, Crypto, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:** India's currency-in-circulation to GDP ratio is lower than the pre-demonetisation level despite an increase in currency with the public.
**Statement II:** India has witnessed a faster increase in the size of the economy due to strong GDP growth of above 6% annually.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct,
but Statement–II does **not** explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **currency-in-circulation to GDP ratio** shows how much cash people hold relative to the size of the economy. The currency-to-GDP ratio fell to 11.11% in 2025, lower than the 12.1% pre-demonetisation level despite an increase in currency with the public from ₹17.97 lakh crore in November 2016 to ₹37.29 lakh crore in October 2025.  **So, statement I is correct.**
● From the graph given above it is clear that **India's GDP has mostly grown above 6 percent annually** with the exception of 2019-20 and 2020-21. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The strong **GDP growth** of above 6% annually **increased the size of the economy much faster than the** **rise in currency** with the public. As a result, even though the absolute amount of cash doubled, the **currency-to-GDP ratio fell**, making it lower than the pre-demonetisation level. **So, Statement-II** **correctly explains Statement-I.** **So, both Statement–I and Statement–II are correct and Statement–II explains Statement–I.**
**Relevance:** Currency with the public has risen from ₹17.97 lakh crore in November 2016 to ₹37.29 lakh crore in October 2025, more than doubling since demonetisation.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.microTopic: Allied Sectors (Animal Husbandry, Horticulture, Sericulture, Apiculture, Aquaculture)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements about the Marine Fisheries Census (MFC) 2025:
I. - It uses a fully digital mode of data collection for the first time.
II.	 It will compile detailed socio-economic
profiles of fisher households for the first time.
III.	 It provides a digital platform for registering
fishers under the Blue Revolution Integrated Development and Management Fisheries Scheme.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer:** **a**
**Explanation:**
● The **National Marine Fisheries Census (MFC) 2025**, launched on 31st October 2025, represents a significant advancement toward **fully digital and geo-referenced data collection in India’s fisheries** **sector.** It was conducted by the **Department of Fisheries** under the **Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada** **Yojana (PMMSY),** with **ICAR-Central Marine Fisheries Research Institute (CMFRI)** as the nodal agency and Fishery Survey of India (FSI) as the operational partner. This edition marks a major technological shift **as the first fully digitised data collection in the history of Indian fisheries. So,** **statement I is correct.**
● MFC 2025 adopts an advanced digital ecosystem supported by custom mobile applications, including **VyAS–NAV, VyAS–BHARAT and VyAS–SUTRA,** which enable real-time, geo-referenced enumeration, immediate data verification and continuous monitoring of field operations. For the first time, the **Census will generate detailed socio-economic profiles of fisher households,** including information
on income, insurance status, access to credit and participation in government schemes. **So, statement II** **is correct.**
● A key innovation is the Census’s integration with the **National Fisheries Digital Platform (NFDP),** allowing fishers and fish farmers to **digitally register** for benefits under the **Pradhan Mantri Matsya** **Kisan Samridhi Sah-Yojana (PM-MKSSY).** This digital linkage is expected to improve transparency, simplify welfare delivery, and strengthen the formulation of targeted, climate-resilient, and inclusive fisheries policies. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● India stands as the **second-largest fish-producing country globally,** accounting for nearly **8 per cent** of the world’s fish output.
● Between 2013–14 and 2024–25, India’s total fish production more than doubled, increasing from **96 lakh tonnes to 195 lakh tonnes**, with inland fisheries registering an impressive 140 percent growth during this period.
● Seafood exports now amount to Rs. 62,408 crore in 2024-25, reflecting the sector’s rising global competitiveness.
● **Blue Revolution Integrated Development and Management Fisheries Scheme,** or Blue Revolution Scheme, was launched in **FY2015-16**.
o The scheme primarily focused on increasing fish production and productivity.
● **Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)** was launched in 2020. It is being implemented for a period of five years (2020-21 to 2024-25) with an investment of Rs 20,050 crore**.**
● **ReALCRaft** is a web-based application developed using **open-source technology** that enables the online registration of fishing vessels and the issuance of fishing licenses. The platform allows applicants to submit their applications remotely and complete the entire process digitally, including **e-payments.**
**Relevance:** Union Minister of State for Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying launched the household enumeration phase of the National Marine Fisheries Census (MFC) 2025 at the ICAR-Central Marine Fisheries Research Institute (CMFRI).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
Silicon Carbide (SiC), which was recently in the news with regard to semiconductor manufacturing in India, is known for which of the following characteristics?
I. - Low thermal conductivity
II.	 High hardness and wear resistance
III.	 Low density
IV.	 High thermal expansion
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Silicon carbide (SiC)** is defined as a hard, covalently bonded ceramic material composed of silicon and carbon atoms. Silicon carbide (SiC) can be manufactured through reaction bonding or sintering, and the chosen method significantly influences the resulting microstructure.
● In **reaction-bonded SiC,** a mixture of SiC and carbon is compacted and then infiltrated with molten silicon. The silicon reacts with carbon to form additional SiC, which binds the original particles.
● **Sintered SiC,** on the other hand, is made from **high-purity SiC powder blended with non-oxide** **sintering aids** and formed using conventional ceramic methods. It is densified in an inert atmosphere at temperatures approaching or exceeding 2000°C.
● Both reaction-bonded and sintered SiC exhibit exceptional wear resistance and robust mechanical performance, including high-temperature capability and thermal shock resistance.
● **The following are the key characteristics of Silicon Carbide:** ○ **Low density. So, point III is correct.** ○ High strength ○ High-temperature strength (reaction-bonded SiC) ○ Oxidation resistance (reaction-bonded SiC) ○ Excellent thermal shock resistance ○ **High hardness and wear resistance. So, point II is correct.** ○ Exceptional chemical resistance ○ **Low thermal expansion with high thermal conductivity. So, point IV and I are not correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box** **The following are the major applications of Silicon Carbide:**
● Turbine components (fixed and moving)
● Seals, bearings, pump vanes
● Ball valve components
● Wear plates
● Kiln furniture
● Heat exchangers
● Semiconductor wafer processing equipment
**Relevance:** Recently, India launched an end-to-end silicon carbide semiconductor plant in Odisha.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: International Organizations (UN, IMF, World Bank, ASEAN, BRICS, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to the UNESCO Creative Cities Network :
I. - It recognises and safeguards both tangible and intangible cultural heritage.
II.	 The UNESCO Mondiacult 2022 declaration
acknowledges culture as a ‘global public good’.
III.	 Recently, Lucknow has been declared
a UNESCO Creative City under the Gastronomy category.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: d**
**Explanation**:
● The **Creative Cities Network** was launched by UNESCO in 2004 to support cities that leverage culture and creativity as drivers of development. The UNESCO Creative Cities Network covers eight creative fields: Architecture, Crafts and Folk Art, Design, Film, Gastronomy, Literature, Media Arts, and Music. **So, statement I is correct**.
● The UNESCO World Conference on Cultural Policies and Sustainable Development – **Mondiacult 2022**, hosted by the government of Mexico, recognised culture’s strategic role and reaffirmed it as a “**global** **public good**.” The MONDIACULT 2022 Declaration calls for the systemic integration of culture into public policies at the local level, emphasising the importance of engaging and mobilising local stakeholders and partners, particularly through the UNESCO Creative Cities Network. **So, statement II** **is correct**.
● **Lucknow** has been added to the list of UNESCO creative cities for its rich and diverse **culinary heritage**. The city has been recognised in the category of Gastronomy at the 43rd session of its General Conference in **Samarkand, Uzbekistan**. The designation places Lucknow among 70 gastronomy cities worldwide and makes it the **second Indian city** to win the title after **Hyderabad.** **So, statement III is correct.**
**Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance**: Recently, Lucknow has officially been declared a UNESCO ‘Creative City of Gastronomy’ during the 43rd Session of the UNESCO General Conference.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Space
questions.questionText: Which one of the following best describes Alfven waves’?

options.a:
Pressure waves generated by convection that
heat the Sun’s upper photosphere
options.b:
Low-frequency, transverse electromagnetic
waves travelling along the sun’s magnetic field
options.c:
Shock waves produced during solar flares
that compress plasma outward
options.d:
High-frequency, longitudinal waves travelling
along the solar convective zone

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: b** **Explanation**:
● Alfven waves are **low-frequency, transverse electromagnetic waves** that travel along the Sun’s magnetic field lines. They play a key role in accelerating charged particles and heating plasma to high energies.
● These waves appeared as twisting patterns in the corona’s magnetic field, suggesting their widespread presence throughout the Sun’s atmosphere. These waves likely carry significant energy, an important finding in the ongoing debate over how the Sun’s corona gets so hot.
**Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance**: A recent study conducted by solar physicist Richard Morton at Northumbria University examined “magnetic waves” in the Sun’s atmosphere that might account for the heating of the Sun’s corona.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Tiger Reserve, Elephant Reserve, other species-specific reserves, etc.
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to Nagarjunasagar Srisailam Tiger Reserve:
I. - It extends over Rajiv Gandhi Wildlife Sanctuary and Gundla Brahmeswaram Wildlife Sanctuary.
II.	 It has the largest area designated as “Critical
Tiger Habitat” in the country.
III. The Kaveri River flows through the Tiger
Reserve.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: a**
**Explanation**:
● **Nagarjunasagar Srisailam Tiger Reserve (NSTR)** is located in the Nallamala hill ranges of the Eastern Ghats. NSTR spans the districts of **Guntur, Prakasam**, and **Kurnool** in **Andhra Pradesh**, as well as **Nalgonda** and **Mahabubnagar** in **Telangana**. Two wildlife sanctuaries, namely **Rajiv Gandhi Wildlife** **Sanctuary** and **Gundla Brahmeswaram Wildlife Sanctuary** (GBM), constitute the tiger Reserve. **So,** **statement I is correct**.
● **Critical Tiger Habitat (CTH)**, also known as the core areas of tiger reserves, is designated under the Wildlife Protection Act (WLPA), 1972. The notification of CTH is carried out by the state government in consultation with the expert committee formed for this purpose. NSTR has a total area of 5937 square kilometres, of which 3721 square kilometres is the core area, making it the **largest CTH** in the country**.** **So, statement II is correct.**
● The **Krishna River** flows through the NSTR Tiger Reserve for approximately 270 kilometres. The **Nallamala hills** feature several plateaus, including Amrabad, Srisailam, Peddacheruvu, Sivapuram, and Nekkanto. The reserve is home to many waterfalls, such as **Ethpothala Falls**, **Pedda Dukudu**, **Gundam**, and **Chaleswaram**. **So, statement III is not correct.**
● The ancient temples at Srisailam, dedicated to **Lord Malikarjuna** and his consorts, **Goddess** **Bhramaramba (an incarnation of Parvati)**, contain one of the twelve sacred Jyothilingas and one of the **eighteen Mahasakthi Peethas** in India.
● The region also hosts the ruins of **Nagarjuna Viswa Vidyalayam**, once a centre for Buddhist universities and monasteries. Additionally, it features several rock shelters and cave temples, including **Akka** **Mahadevi Bhilam**, **Dattatreya Bhilam**, **Umaa Maheshwaram**, **Kadalivanam**, and **Palankasari**.
**Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● **The sixth edition of All India Tiger Estimation 2025-26** will take place in three phases between December and April 2026. In the first phase, the field staff will traverse 673 designated blocks and record evidence of tigers and other carnivores, herbivores and habitat quality using the **M-StrIPES (Monitoring System for** **Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status)** mobile application.
● The second phase will involve analysis of data collected during the initial fieldwork, while the third phase will involve camera trapping. **For the first time,** **tigers will be counted outside the corridors of wildlife sanctuaries across the** **country in this census**.
● **The previous national tiger census, conducted in 2022, recorded 3,682 tigers** **across India**.
**Relevance:** The forest department is preparing to launch the next round of tiger census in the Nagarjuna Sagar Tiger Reserve (NSTR).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText: The term ‘enshittification’, sometimes seen in the news, refers to:

options.a: The rise of AI-enabled chatbots on the internet
options.c:
Reduction of attention span due to excess
screen time
options.d:
Degradation in the quality and user experience
of online platforms

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: d**
**Explanation**:
● The term ‘**enshittification**’ was coined in 2022 by Canada-born author, tech journalist, and activist **Cory** **Doctorow**, describing the **degradation in the quality and experience of online platforms** over time, often due to profit-seeking behaviour or monopolistic control.
● Examples include the addition of advertisements, self-preferencing by tech companies, unfair bias in search results, the conversion of free features into paid ones, the replacement of genuine products with cheaper imitations, and more.
**Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance**: The now-viral term ‘enshittification’ has helped put a name to a change that internet users are noticing: the feeling that many of their digital experiences, transactions, and services are not improving over time.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: National Park, Wildlife Sanctuary, Biosphere Reserve
questions.questionText:
Consider the following information:
Protected Area
State Feature
I. - Khangc­ hendzonga National Park
Sikkim India's first “Mixed World Heritage Site
II. - Netravali Wildlife Sanctuary
Kerala Zuari River originates here
III. - Nagarhole tiger re­ serve
Karnataka Acts as an ‘ecological confluence’ of the Western and Eastern Ghats.
IV. - Sariska Wildlife Sanctuary
Rajasthan World’s first reserve to successfully relocate tigers after local extinction
In how many of the above rows is the given information correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Khangchendzonga National Park** is located in **Sikkim** state of India. Recently the **IUCN rated it as** **‘good’**, making it the **only Indian site** to receive this top rating in the latest global review of natural World Heritage Sites. The park is India’s **first mixed UNESCO World Heritage Site**, known for its glaciers, rich wildlife such as snow leopards and red pandas. It also has cultural significance for the Lepcha and Buddhist communities.  **So, pair I is correctly matched**
● **Netravali Wildlife Sanctuary** is in **Goa** and not in Kerala. It also connects ecologically to Karnataka’s Kali Tiger Reserve. **River Netravali or Neturli originates in this sanctuary** which is an important tributary of the Zuari River. To the north of the Netravali wildlife sanctuary lies the Bhagwan Mahavir Wildlife Sanctuary and the Cotigao Wildlife Sanctuary lies to its south. **So, pair II is not correctly** **matched**
○ Recently the Supreme Court-appointed Central Empowered Committee (CEC) recommended including Netravali, along with Cotigao Wildlife Sanctuary, as part of a new tiger reserve in Goa.
● **Nagarhole Tiger Reserve** is located in **Karnataka** state of India at the tri junction of the States of Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala. It is geographically known as an **‘ecological confluence’ of the** **Western and Eastern Ghats.** The reserve forms part of the larger Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve and supports rich biodiversity, including large populations of tigers and Asian elephants. **So, pair III is correctly** **matched**
● Sariska Wildlife Sanctuary is located in the state of Rajasthan.  Sariska is known as the **world’s first** **reserve to successfully relocate tigers** after they went locally extinct due to poaching in 2004. The sanctuary is part of the **Aravalli landscape** and is known for its dry deciduous forests, historic temples, and  tiger population. **So, pair IV is correctly matched**
○ Recently, its area was **significantly expanded** to improve ecological connectivity and comply with a **Supreme Court order** related to Critical Tiger Habitat (CTH) boundaries. **So, only three of the above pairs are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer**.
**Relevance:** IUCN recently rated Khangchendzonga National Park as “good” in its latest global review of natural World Heritage sites.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: International Organizations (UN, IMF, World Bank, ASEAN, BRICS, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the International Maritime Organisation (IMO):
I. - It is a specialized agency of the United Nations.
II.	 Its regulatory framework contributes to
the achievement of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goal 14.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The International Maritime Organization (IMO)** **is a specialized agency of the United Nations.** It was established by means of a Convention adopted under the auspices of the United Nations in Geneva on 6 March 1948 and met for the first time in January 1959. **So, statement I is correct.**
● As a specialized agency of the United Nations, IMO is the global standard-setting authority for the **safety,** **security and environmental performance of international shipping.** Its main role is to create a regulatory framework for the shipping industry that is fair and effective, universally adopted and universally implemented. It has an integral role in meeting the targets set out in United Nations Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 14: Conserve and sustainably use the oceans, seas and marine resources for sustainable development. **So, statement II is correct.**
● IMO currently has 176 Member States. There are 66 intergovernmental organizations which have observer status with IMO; and  85 international non-governmental organizations in consultative status with IMO.
● The **IMO Assembly consists of all Member States and is the highest governing body of the** **Organization**. It is responsible for approving the work programme, voting the budget and determining the IMO’s financial arrangements.
● The IMO Council is elected by the Assembly for terms of two years. It acts as the Executive Organ of IMO and is responsible, under the Assembly, for supervising the work of the Organization. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, India has been re-elected to the International Maritime Organisation Council, the premier body that oversees the world's maritime sector.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: Employment
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the recent reforms in Code on Wages (2019):
I. - The Code provides a statutory right to minimum wages for all employees, covering both the organised and unorganised sectors.
II. For the first time, non-imprisonable offences
under the Code can be compounded by paying a penalty.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● In order to simplify and strengthen the framework governing workers’ rights, the Government consolidated 29 labour laws into four comprehensive Labour Codes- namely, the **Code on Wages, 2019,** the Industrial Relations Code, 2020, the Code on Social Security, 2020 and the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The **Code on Wages (2019) provides a statutory right to minimum wages for all employees**, covering both **organised and unorganised sectors.** It replaces multiple earlier laws (such as the Minimum Wages Act, 1948) and ensures minimum wages are universally applicable, removing the concept of scheduled employment. The code requires the central government to fix a floor wage below which no state government can set minimum wages. This expands protection to many workers earlier outside the ambit of wage laws. **So, statement II is correct.**
● For the first time under the labour codes, certain offences that are **not punishable with imprisonment** **can be compounded by paying a prescribed penalty.** This provision is meant to ease compliance and reduce prolonged litigation for minor violations. Such non-imprisonable offences are specified and can be compounded on payment of a prescribed penalty, encouraging faster resolution. **So, statement III is** **correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the government implemented new labour codes, replacing 29 existing laws.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to the G20 Summit 2025, consider
the following statements:
I. - It was held in Johannesburg, South Africa.
II.	 It launched “Mission 300,” aimed at
providing electricity to 300 million people in Sub‑Saharan Africa by 2030.
III.	 It endorsed a non-binding Critical Minerals
Framework.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **G20, or the Group of Twenty,** is an informal grouping of 19 countries (Argentina, Australia, Brazil, Canada, China, France, Germany, India, Indonesia, Italy, Japan, Republic of Korea, Mexico, Russia, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Turkey, United Kingdom, and the United States) and the European Union and African Union.
● The 2025 Summit was held in **Johannesburg, South Africa**. This is the first time a G20 Summit is being held on the African continent. **So, statement I is correct.**
● “Mission 300” is a **World Bank Group and African Development Bank initiative** to connect 300 million people in Sub‑Saharan Africa to electricity by 2030, organized around the Mission 300 **Africa** **Energy Summit** held in Dar es Salaam in January 2025. It was **not launched at the G20** Johannesburg Summit. But G20 supports tripling renewable capacity and Mission 300 to connect 300 million people to electricity by 2030. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● In its 2025 summit declaration, the **G20 emphasised the establishment of the G20 Critical Minerals** **Framework,** aimed at leveraging critical minerals as a catalyst for sustainable development, inclusive economic growth, and resilience. The framework seeks to ensure that mineral-producing countries, particularly in the Global South, derive maximum benefit from their resources, moving beyond mere raw material exports. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the G20 Summit 2025 was held in Johannesburg, South Africa.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Security and Strategic Issues (Terrorism, Cyber, Maritime, Exercises, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Which of the following best describes the Military-
Industrial-Complex (MIC)?

options.a: A global alliance between national governments  and defence industries to reduce military expenditure
options.b:
An industrial unit where military officers and
scientists collaborate to produce the most advanced weapons
options.c:
A collaboration between the armed forces and
private defence companies for early transfer of technology
options.d:
A network of military institutions, defence
industries, policymakers, and technical elites involved in the production of military goods driven by vested interests

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The idea of the **Military-Industrial-Complex (MIC)** was introduced by U.S. President Dwight D. Eisenhower in 1961, when he warned against the  rising influence of  **MIC** over national policy**.**
● The MIC is a **coalition of vested interests** that **benefits when defence budgets increase.** This **network** includes the **armed forces, private defence manufacturers, political actors, and research institutions** that p**roduce military technology.** Because their **profits, power, or influence expand** with rising military spending, they may push governments toward higher defence expenditure even beyond what is necessary for national security.  This coalition could influence policy decisions in ways that were in the interest of its members, but not necessarily in the interests of broader national security. Therefore Eisenhower sought the governments to guard against the unwarranted influence by the military-industrialcomplex. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The global military spending in 2024 stood at a record high of $2.7 trillion an increase of 9.4 per cent,
which points to Military-Industrial-Complex (MIC) for its possible reasons.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:** Passengers can access the Internet while travelling on commercial aircraft.
**Statement II:** In aircrafts, the Internet is provided through satellite-based or air-to-ground communication links.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
but Statement II does **not** explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct but Statement II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **In-flight Internet** refers to wireless **Internet access** provided to **passengers on aircraft,** using onboard **Wi-Fi networks** that relay data to the ground through specialised aviation communication systems. It connects passengers to the web using either **satellite links** or **air-to-ground (ATG)** communication systems. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **In the ATG system,** the aeroplane connects to special cellular-like towers on the ground. These towers have antennae pointing up at the sky. The plane’s antennae — often mounted on the underside — maintains a radio link with whichever tower is currently in range (’radio link’ just means they send and receive electromagnetic signals in the radio frequency range, 3-3,000,000,000 kHz). As the plane flies, the ATG system hands off the connection from one tower to the next, similar to how phone switches between cell towers if you’re on a call in a moving vehicle.
● **Satellite systems are more flexible** because they don’t care whether an aeroplane is over land or water. In this system, the aeroplane uses a dish or phased-array antenna, usually inside a bespoke hump-shaped structure on top of the fuselage, that’s pointed towards the sky. This antenna will transmit data to a satellite in orbit, which will relay the data to a ground station connected to the regular Internet. **So, statement II** **is correct.** **So, both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I**
**Relevance:** With rising global air travel, in-flight Internet has become a standard service on commercial flights, prompting renewed public interest in how aircraft stay connected mid-air.
