questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Term recently seen in the news
Description
I. - ReALCRaft Portal To provide a has­ sle-free travel experi­ ence to air travellers across airports in India.
II. - CE20 A cryogenic engine developed by the In­ dian Space Research Organisation.
III. - ANNA DARPAN Microservices-based integrated supply chain management system
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **ReALCRaft portal** is a national online platform developed by the Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying.
○ It provides web-based, citizen-centric services to marine fishers and coastal States/UTs **for the** **registration and licensing of fishing vessels**, transfer of ownership, and related processes. **So,** **pair I is not correctly matched.**
● The **Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)** has recently achieved a major breakthrough with the successful testing of **boot-strap mode start on a cryogenic engine, CE20.** The CE20 cryogenic engine will propel the Gaganyaan mission. This marks the first time Isro has achieved a boot-strap start for a gasgenerator cycle cryogenic engine without relying on any auxiliary stored-gas system. **So, pair II is** **correctly matched.**
○ CE-20 powers the upper stage of the LVM3, with a lift-off capacity of 4,000 tonnes. This is a three-stage launch vehicle: two solid strap-on motors carrying up to 204 tonnes of solid propellant; one liquid core stage with twin engines carrying 115 tonnes of liquid propellant, and lastly, the high-thrust cryogenic upper stage, carrying 28 tonnes of propellant.
● The Food Corporation of India (FCI) has introduced **ANNA DARPAN**. It is a solution for managing the end to end operations of FCI Depots. It is a new **microservices-based integrated supply chain**
**management system.** The system is being designed to streamline and enhance the entire supply chain operations and services at various levels including Mandis, Mills, Depots**. So, pair III is correctly** **matched.**
○ It connects key activities such as procurement, storage, movement, sales, quality checks, labour management and contract monitoring under one integrated system. It is aimed at modernizing warehousing operations, improving supply chain efficiency and enhancing transparency across the Public Distribution System (PDS). **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, ISRO successfully tests boot-strap mode start on CE20 cryogenic engine, enhancing future LVM3 mission flexibility and restart capability.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.microTopic: Extra-Constitutional Bodies
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Central Pay Commission, consider the following statements:
I. - It is a non-statutory and non-constitutional body.
II. - The first Pay Commission was established before the independence of India.
III. 	It is responsible for salary revisions of all the
central government employees except the defence personnels.
IV. 	The Central Government is not bound to
accept the recommendations of the Pay Commission.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Central Pay Commission** is a committee established by the Government of India under the provisions of **Article 309 of the Constitution. They are set up in India by an executive order based** **on a Cabinet decision** at regular intervals to revise salaries and pensions of government employees to ensure its alignment with inflation and economic conditions. Article 309 allows the President to make rules regulating the recruitment and the conditions of service of persons appointed to the public services of the Union. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **The first Pay Commission was established in January 1946, even before independence,** to rationalise the pay structure of colonial civil services. Since then, India has constituted seven Pay Commissions., approximately once in every decade. **So, statement II is correct.**
● Its primary mandate is to review and recommend changes in the salary structure, allowances, and pension schemes of central government employees, **including defense personnel**. It recommends changes for central government employees and pensioners **as well as the defence forces. So, statement III is not** **correct.**
● **It is not mandatory for the government to accept the recommendations of the pay commission.** The government may choose to accept or reject them. But they do not generally ignore them. Historical data suggest near-universal adoption. All states and UTs implemented the 7th CPC within 2-3 years, often with state-specific fitment factors. Fitment factor is a multiplier used to calculate the revised basic pay. **So,** **statement IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The Centre has officially constituted the 8th Central Pay Commission.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Defence
questions.questionText:
Which of the following countries has recently
approved the sale of anti-tank Javelin Missile System to India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The United States has recently approved the possible sale of the Javelin missile system**, Excalibur projectiles, and related equipment at a total estimated cost of $92.8 million.

India has recently signed a defence deal with the United States for a sustainment package of the Indian Navy’s MH-60R Seahawk helicopter fleet. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** The United States recently approved a possible sale of the Javelin Missile System to India.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Biology and Biotechnology
questions.questionText:
With reference to the gene-editing technology
based on Transposon-associated protein (TnpB), recently developed by ICAR, consider the following statements:
I. - TnpB functions as a programmable nuclease capable of precise, targeted genome editing.
II.	 The technology has potential applications in
crop improvement and plant biotechnology.
III.	 TnpB is a larger than CRISPR-Cas systems,
which enhances its editing efficiency.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Scientists at the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) have developed a “miniature alternative” to the proprietary CRISPR-Cas proteins-based technology to precisely cut and tweak the DNA of plants. ICAR has recently been granted a patent for this technology. It provides a new tool for genome editing in plants.
● The new gene editing technology **deploys the so-called TnpB or Transposon-associated proteins**. **These proteins act as “molecular scissors” to cleave the DNA of a gene at a predetermined target** **site** and change its sequence. Such editing is aimed at bringing forth desirable alterations in that gene’s expression and function. **So, statement I is correct.**
● It could be used in genome-edited (GE) crop breeding. It could be used for the development of new genetically modified crop plants. **So, statement II is correct.**
● **The advantage with the TnpB proteins is their compactness.** **They are much smaller**, having 400-500 amino acids per molecule, **compared with 1,000-1,400 for Cas9 and about 1,300 for Cas12a. So,** **statement III is not correct.**
○ The bulkiness of Cas9 and Cas12a makes it more challenging to deliver these proteins into the cells where the targeted DNA/genetic material resides. ○ Small cargo size enables the protein to be easily packed in a viral vector that can be directly injected into the cell, and thus tissue culture-mediated delivery isn’t required. The transfer of Cas proteins is done via agrobacterium tissue culture media. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Scientists at the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) have developed a new gene editing technique.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Earthquake and Volcanism
questions.questionText:
With reference to volcanic eruptions, consider the
following statements:
I. - Magma with higher silica content has greater viscosity, leading to more explosive eruptions with ash-dominated deposits.
II.	 Magma with higher gas content tends to
produce gentle eruptions with steady lava flows.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Volcanic eruptions** vary from **gentle lava outpourings** to **violent explosions,** depending primarily on **magma viscosity** (controlled by silica content) and dissolved gas content. Silica-rich, **sticky magma** **traps gases,** whereas silica-poor magma allows gases to escape, influencing eruption style.
● **Silica-rich magma** is highly viscous (“sticky”), **preventing gases from escaping** easily. As **pressure** **builds** within the magma chamber, it eventually releases violently, producing **explosive eruptions** **dominated by ash, pyroclasts**, and towering plumes, like the recent **Hayli Gubbi** event. **So, statement** **I is correct.**
● **Higher gas** content does not lead to gentle eruptions. Instead, more dissolved gases **increase internal** **pressure**—much like shaking a soda bottle—driving **explosive eruptions.** Gentle, **smooth lava flows** occur in low-gas, silica-poor magma, such as that in **Hawaiian volcanoes. So, statement II is not correct.**
**Knowledge Box**
**About Hayli Gubbi Volcano**
● It is located in **Ethiopia’s Afar region** about 800km northeast of Addis Ababa near the **Eritrean border.**
● It is a **shield volcano** that sits within Ethiopia’s **Danakil Depression,** one of the hottest and most remote areas on the planet.
● It is the southernmost volcano in the **Erta Ale range.** The Erta Ale range is known for its **persistent lava lakes** and is primarily made up of shield volcanoes.
**Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Hayli Gubbi volcano erupted for the first time after 10,000 years.

questions.subject: History
questions.microTopic: National and Regional Festivals
questions.questionText:
With reference to the festivals celebrated in
different regions of India, consider the following pairs:
Festival State
I. - Ramman Festival Uttarakhand
II. - Bali Yatra Festival Odisha
III. - Sangai Festival Manipur
How many of the pairs given above is **not** correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Ramman Festival is a traditional festival celebrated in villages of Saloor-Dungra in Uttarakhand,** annually in late April during Baisakhi. It is dedicated to the tutelary god, Bhumiyal Devta, a local divinity whose temple houses most of the festivities. **So, pair I is correctly matched.**
○ The festival involves theatrical performances of the Ramayana and local legends, in which people sing songs and wear masks while dancing. There are 18 different types of masks made of Bhojpatra, Himalayan birch, that performers wear during the event. ○ In 2009, Ramman was inscribed in the list of UNESCO’s Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.
● **Bali Yatra is a nine-day festival held annually in Odisha.** It is the symbol of Odisha’s glorious maritime commercial tradition and cultural heritage. It is held annually on the banks of the river Mahanadi. It begins on Kartik Purnima (full moon night in the month of Kartik). **So, pair II is correctly matched.**
● **Sangai festival is the annual tourism festival of Manipur.** It takes its name from the Sangai, the rare brow-antlered deer found exclusively in Manipur. **It is a ten-day extravaganza** that immerses visitors into the art, music, handicrafts, indigenous sports, cuisine, and adventure sports of the region. **So, pair III** **is correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Sangai festival was recently celebrated in Manipur.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: International Organisations (WTO, IMF, World Bank, RCEP, FTAs, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
WorldSkills Asia:
I. - It is an Asian Development Bank initiative to promote quality technical skills and vocational education.
II. India secured the 8th rank in its first-ever
participation at the World Skills.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Answer: b** **Explanation**:
● **WorldSkills Asia** is an **international non-profit organisation** of Asian member countries and regions organised to promote quality technical skills and vocational education. Focusing on the **four major pillars** of set programs: Promoting Skills, Education and Training, International Cooperation and Skills Competitions, WorldSkills Asia is envisioned to be the Asian Hub for skills excellence and development geared towards making a better world through the power of skills. **So, statement I is not correct**.
● **Taipei** hosted the **3rd WorldSkills Asia Competition** from November 27 to November 29, 2025, at Nangang Exhibition Centre.
● **India** sent a 23-member contingent to the WorldSkills Asia, marking the **first time** **India is participating in** **the WorldSkills Asia platform**. India marked a significant milestone on the global skills stage by **securing** **8th rank in its first-ever participation** at the World Skills Asia Competition 2025, competing against 29 nations. In the competition, India won one Silver, two Bronze medals and three Medallions for Excellence in both traditional and tech-driven skill categories. **So, statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.** **Relevance**: Recently, India has participated in the WorldSkills Asia platform for the first time.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.microTopic: Governance and Policies
questions.questionText:
With reference to Constitution Day, observed on
26 November in India, consider the following statements:
I. - The Government of India formally notified the observance of Constitution Day in 2015.
II.	 The date marks the adoption of the Poorna
Swaraj resolution by the Indian National Congress.
III.	 Recently, editions of the Constitution were
released in Bodo and Kashmiri languages.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Samvidhan Divas, or Constitution Day of India,** is observed annually on 26 November to mark the adoption of the Constitution by the Constituent Assembly in 1949.
● The **Poorna Swaraj resolution was passed on 26 January 1930,** not on 26 November, which is why India celebrates Republic Day on 26 January each year. Constitution Day, therefore, has no connection with the Poorna Swaraj resolution. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● In **2015, the Government of India officially declared 26 November as Constitution Day,** designating the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment as the nodal ministry for its observance. **So, statement** **I is correct.**
● The **Legislative Department has released the Constitution in nine regional languages**: Malayalam, Marathi, Nepali, Punjabi, **Bodo, Kashmiri**, Telugu, Odia, and Assamese, to enhance accessibility and promote greater constitutional awareness. The Bodo and Kashmiri editions were released for the first time. This initiative aims to ensure that citizens can engage with the Constitution in their native languages, making its principles and provisions more inclusive and widely understood. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, Constitution Day in India 2025 was celebrated.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.microTopic: Governance and Policies
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements regarding the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile:
I. - It is a two-stage missile with a solid propellant booster engine as its first stage.
II. - It has the ability to strike from land, sea, and air platforms.
III. Philippines is the first country to procure the
weapon system from India.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **BrahMos is a two-stage missile with a solid propellant booster engine as its first stage** which brings it to supersonic speed and then gets separated. The **liquid ramjet or the second stage** then takes the missile closer to 2.8 Mach speed in cruise phase. Stealth technology and guidance system with advanced embedded software provides the missile with special features. The missile has a flight range of up to 290 km with supersonic speed all through the flight, leading to shorter flight time, consequently ensuring lower dispersion of targets, quicker engagement time and non-interception by any known weapon system in the world.  **So, statement I is correct.**
● It operates on **‘Fire and Forget' principle**, adopting varieties of flight paths on its way to the target. The missile's destructive power is enhanced due to the large kinetic energy on impact. Its cruising altitude could be up to 15 km and terminal altitude is as low as 5 meters. BRAHMOS carries a conventional warhead upto 200 kgs. The missile has **identical configuration for land, sea and sub-sea platforms** and uses a Transport Launch Canister (TLC) for transportation, storage and launch. The canister for the missile serves the dual purpose of a container (to store & transport the missile) and also that of a launching tube. **So, statement II is correct.**
● It has carved its global footprint by becoming India’s first full-scale weapon to receive a historic export contract from the Republic of the Philippines for the procurement of a shore-based anti-ship missile system.
● India and Indonesia recently discussed the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile procurement, during the third India-Indonesia Defence Ministers’ Dialogue. This marks further progress towards the sale of the weapon system to Jakarta.
● If the BrahMos deal goes through, **Indonesia will be the second country to procure the weapon system** **from India, after the Philippines** which has procured three batteries of weapon system in 2022. The missiles procured by the Philippines have a range of around 290 km.  **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** India and Indonesia discussed the finer details related to BrahMos supersonic cruise missile procurement, during the third India-Indonesia Defence Ministers’ Dialogue held recently.

questions.subject: History
questions.microTopic: GI Tags, Awards & Honours
questions.questionText:
Which of the following has/have been accorded ‘Geographical Indication’ status?
I. - Indi Lime
II. - Puliyankudi Lime
III. Gondia Lime
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A Geographical Indication is a form of **intellectual property right** used for products that have a specific geographical origin and possess qualities, reputation or characteristics essentially attributable to that place. In India, GI protection is governed by the **Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and** **Protection) Act, 1999**, and registration gives producers in that region the **exclusive legal right to use the** **GI name** and prevents misuse or imitation by others.
● Indi Lime from Vijayapura, Karnataka, **received a GI tag in 2023** for its **Kagzi variety**, known for its high juice content, zesty aroma, and balanced acidity. It has also been part of GI-tagged export consignments to the UK. **So, point I is correct.**
● **Puliyankudi Lime** from the Puliyankudi–Kadayam region of Tenkasi district, **Tamil Nadu**, **received a** **GI tag in April 2025**. Known for its thin peel, strong acidity, and high vitamin C content, it offers an impressive **juice yield of about 55%.** **So, point II is correct.**
● **Gondia Lime is not listed among registered GI products** in India, whereas other citrus fruits, such as Nagpur Orange and Assam Lemon, are among the other GI-tagged products. **So, point III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, India exported its first-ever GI-tagged Indi and Puliyankudi limes to the UK.

questions.questionText:
With reference to Natural Farming and Organic Farming, consider the following statements:
I. - Unlike Organic Farming, Natural Farming advocates a minimalist approach and completely discourages the use of external inputs.
II. - Zero tillage is a mandatory and defining practice under Organic Farming systems, whereas it is not integral to Natural Farming.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Although, both organic and natural farming systems are non-chemical systems of farming largely relying on diversity, on-farm biomass management, rejuvenation of natural nutrient recycling, crop rotation, multiple cropping and efficient resource recycling but with following differences:
○ **Organic systems** in addition to above are **open to use of off-farm purchased organic and** **biological inputs** and need based soil correction through natural mined minerals. ○ **Natural farming systems** are based on biomass mulching, round the year green cover, indigenous cow-based dung and urine formulations in e**xclusion of all purchased inputs organic, biological** **or otherwise**. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Zero tillage (no-till) in Natural Farming is central**, emphasizing no soil disturbance, relying on mulching (crop residue), diverse cropping, and natural amendments to build soil life, **while in Organic** **Farming, no-till is an option**, often used but sometimes replaced by mechanical weeding or cover crops termination (mowing/rolling) to manage weeds without herbicides, focusing on overall system health rather than strict no-disturbance. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● The Government of India is promoting both these non-chemical systems of agriculture. Natural farming, through **National Mission on Natural Farming** (NMNF) and organic farming through **Paramparagat** **Krishi Vikas Yojna** (PKVY) and **Mission Organic Value Chain Development for North Eastern** **Region** (MOVCDNER). **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, the Prime Minister of India has inaugurated the three-day South India Natural Farming Summit 2025 in Tamil Nadu.

questions.questionText: Sentinel-6B, which was recently in the news, is a joint mission between:

options.a: National Aeronautics and Space Administration and Indian Space Research Organisation
options.b:
United Nations Environment Programme
and International Union for Conservation of Nature
options.c:
SpaceX and National Aeronautics and Space
Administration
options.d:
European Organisation for the Exploitation
of Meteorological Satellites and National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Sentinel-6B** is an ocean-tracking satellite with six onboard science instruments that will measure the rising sea levels and its impacts on the planet. Sentinel-6B will orbit Earth at a speed of 7.2 km per second, completing one revolution every 112 minutes.
● It is a **collaboration between National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), European** **Space Agency (ESA), European Organisation for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites** **(EUMETSAT), and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).** The European Commission contributed funding support while France’s space agency CNES (Centre National d’Études Spatiales) provided technical expertise. The mission also marks the first international involvement in Copernicus, the European Union’s Earth Observation Programme. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, Sentinel-6B was launched as an ocean-tracking satellite with six onboard science instruments that will measure the rising sea levels and its impacts on the Earth.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.microTopic: Governance and Policies
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. - Doctrine of Party Autonomy allows parties to
choose the scope, applicable law, rules and procedures that will govern their arbitration.
II. - Doctrine of Severability states that any law
which is inconsistent with Fundamental Rights is not valid.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **doctrine of party autonomy** is a legal principle that grants parties the freedom to choose the rules and procedures for their disputes, especially in arbitration. Party autonomy in arbitration means that the parties to a dispute can agree on how that dispute looks. They **can choose the scope, applicable law,** **rules, and procedures that will govern their arbitration. So, statement I is correct.**
○ Perhaps most fundamentally, party autonomy recognises the parties’ freedom to agree to resolve their dispute through arbitration, as opposed to domestic courts. This means that courts will respect the parties’ autonomy in agreeing to forgo litigation in favour of arbitration when a dispute arises. And, by extension, arbitrators will respect the parties’ autonomy in agreeing to specific scopes, laws, rules, or procedures that apply to the arbitration.
● **The doctrine of severability** is also known as the **doctrine of separability.** It helps in the protection of Fundamental Rights. It is mentioned in **clause (1) of Article 13** of the **Constitution of India** that all laws
which were enforced in India, before the commencement of the Constitution, in so far as they are **inconsistent with the provisions of Fundamental Rights shall, to the extent of that inconsistency, be** **void.** But the whole law or act would not be held invalid, only the provisions of the law or act which are not in consistent with the fundamental rights will be held as void. **So, statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● The **doctrine of eclipse** says that **any law which is inconsistent with Fundamental** **Rights is not valid.** Such a law is not totally dead but is overshadowed by the Fundamental Right. The inconsistency (conflict) between the impugned law and the Fundamental Right can be removed by introducing a constitutional amendment to the relevant fundamental right so that the eclipse vanishes and the entire law becomes valid.
**Relevance:** The Supreme Court recently held that the Doctrine of Party Autonomy is not limitless, and the same is the bedrock of arbitration.
